title: A Step-by-Step Guide to Filing a Letter of Undertaking (LUT) for GST
primary_keyword: how to file lut for gst

author.name: Elena Petrova
author.bio: An international business lawyer by trade, Elena breaks down the complexities of freelance contracts, corporate structures, and international liability. Her goal is to empower freelancers with the legal knowledge to operate confidently.

reviewedBy.name: Dr. Alistair Finch
reviewedBy.bio: With a Ph.D. in Economics and over 15 years at a Big Four accounting firm, Alistair specializes in demystifying cross-border tax law for independent professionals. He focuses on risk mitigation and long-term financial planning.

key_takeaways: Utilize a Letter of Undertaking (LUT) to export services without paying the 18% IGST upfront, ensuring you retain immediate control over your working capital.
Systematize filing a new LUT in the first week of April each year to prevent a compliance gap that invalidates its benefit for any invoices issued before the filing date.

Secure a Foreign Inward Remittance Certificate (FIRC) or Bank Realisation Certificate (BRC) for every overseas transaction to prove payment was received within one year, a mandatory step to validate your LUT's zero-rated tax benefit during an audit.
summary: For Indian professionals exporting services, paying 18% IGST upfront and chasing refunds creates a significant cash flow problem that locks up vital working capital. The article's core advice is to file an annual Letter of Undertaking (LUT), a simple declaration that allows you to export services as "zero-rated supplies" without any upfront tax payment. By adopting this strategy, you gain the key outcome of complete control over your cash, eliminating the burdensome refund process and keeping your money working for your business.
ai_overview_answer: A Letter of Undertaking (LUT) is a declaration filed by Indian service exporters to export services without paying the Integrated GST (IGST) upfront, which is the most effective way to control cash flow. By filing an LUT, you can issue zero-rated invoices to global clients, meaning you do not have to pay the 18% IGST and then claim it back. This immediately unlocks your working capital and completely eliminates the administrative burden of navigating the complex and often delayed government refund process. The LUT is an optional facility for GST-registered exporters and must be filed annually on the GST portal using Form GST RFD-11. To remain compliant, you must receive payment for your services in convertible foreign exchange within one year of the invoice date. Filing an LUT also simplifies your accounting and signals a high degree of operational maturity to international clients. Ultimately, it is a strategic choice that transforms a recurring compliance task into a significant financial advantage.

itemListElement.name: A Step-by-Step Guide to Filing a Letter of Undertaking (LUT) for GST

@graph.headline: A Step-by-Step Guide to Filing a Letter of Undertaking (LUT) for GST
@graph.description: Wondering how to file LUT for GST? Our step-by-step SOP shows you how to export services tax-free, protecting your cash flow. Master the process today.

step.name: Navigate to the Service
step.text: Log in to the official GST Portal. Go to Services > User Services > Furnish Letter of Undertaking (LUT). This direct path takes you exactly where you need to be.

step.name: Select the Financial Year
step.text: The system will display Form GST RFD-11. Your first input is to select the correct financial year from the drop-down list (e.g., '2025-26' for the period from April 2025 to March 2026). An LUT is valid for one financial year.

step.name: Complete the Self-Declaration and Witness Details
step.text: This is where your preparation pays off. Tick the three checkboxes for the self-declaration, affirming your commitment to comply with GST regulations. Next, enter the Name, Occupation, and Address for the two independent witnesses whose details you gathered earlier.

step.name: Sign and File the Application
step.text: The final step is authorization. Select the EVC option, which will trigger an OTP sent to your registered mobile number and email address. Entering this OTP authenticates your identity and officially completes your filing.

step.name: Download Your Acknowledgment
step.text: The moment you file, the portal generates an Application Reference Number (ARN) and displays a confirmation message. Your final, critical action is to click the DOWNLOAD button to save the acknowledgment PDF. This document is your official proof of compliance. Save it immediately in a dedicated folder for your tax records.

mainEntity.name: LUT vs. IGST Refund: Which is better for a service professional?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a service professional whose lifeblood is cash flow, the LUT is definitively the superior option. The alternative—paying 18% IGST upfront and claiming a refund—locks up your working capital for weeks or months. The LUT allows you to export as a "zero-rated supply" from the outset, keeping your capital working for you. Comparison: Feature - Letter of Undertaking (LUT) vs. IGST Payment & Refund. Upfront Cash Outlay - None vs. Required. Working Capital - Protected vs. Blocked. Administrative Effort - Minimal vs. High.

mainEntity.name: What is the time limit for export after filing an LUT?

acceptedAnswer.text: For services, you must receive payment in convertible foreign exchange within one year from the date you issue the export invoice. Failing to meet this deadline for a specific transaction means you must pay the IGST amount plus interest.

mainEntity.name: Can I file an LUT after the financial year begins?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you can file an LUT at any point during the financial year. However, it is only effective from the date of filing. Any export invoices issued before you file the LUT for that year will not be covered, requiring you to pay IGST and claim a refund for those transactions.

mainEntity.name: Who can be a witness for a GST LUT?

acceptedAnswer.text: You need two independent individuals. They simply need to provide their full name, occupation, and address. There is no requirement for them to be a notary or a Chartered Accountant. They can be colleagues, friends, or other professionals not directly associated with your business.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I forget to renew my LUT?

acceptedAnswer.text: If you forget to renew your LUT, any export invoices you issue will not qualify as zero-rated supplies. You will be legally obligated to charge and pay IGST on those services, forcing you into the refund process the LUT is designed to prevent.

mainEntity.name: Is an LUT mandatory for exporting services?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, it is not mandatory. The LUT is an optional facility to simplify compliance. The alternative is to pay the applicable IGST on your export invoice and subsequently file for a refund. The LUT is a strategic choice to improve your financial efficiency.

body.content: Why the LUT is Your Secret Weapon for Cash Flow Control

For an independent professional serving a global clientele, control is everything—control over your projects, your time, and most critically, your cash flow. That control begins not with complex financial engineering, but with one of the most powerful—and overlooked—tools in the Indian tax system for service exporters: the Letter of Undertaking (LUT).

Filing an LUT is a strategic decision to take direct command of your working capital. By leveraging this simple instrument, you shift from a reactive stance—paying tax and then chasing refunds—to a proactive one where you retain your cash from the outset. Let's break down the tangible benefits.

items: **Unlock Your Working Capital:** The most immediate benefit of an LUT is the ability to export services *without* paying the Integrated GST (IGST) upfront. Exports are "zero-rated supplies" under GST law, meaning they leave the country without a tax liability. Without an LUT, you would be forced to pay the 18% IGST and then claim it back. With an LUT, that payment is never made. For a US$10,000 project, that’s US$1,800 that stays in your bank account, available for reinvestment, operational expenses, or a crucial cash buffer. It’s your money, and the LUT ensures it stays that way.

**Eliminate the Refund Scramble:** The alternative to the LUT is a path paved with administrative friction. You would pay the IGST on your invoice, then navigate the often complex and delayed government refund process. This involves filing separate applications, submitting supporting documents, and waiting—sometimes for months—for the funds to be returned. For a business-of-one, this administrative burden is a profound drain on your most finite resource: your time. The LUT completely eliminates this workflow, freeing you from the anxiety of chasing your own money.

**Signal Professionalism to Global Clients:** In the world of high-value consulting, perception is reality. Operating under an LUT signals a high degree of operational maturity. It shows sophisticated corporate clients that you have a professional, legally sound system for managing your tax obligations. This isn't just about Indian tax law; it's a trust signal that resonates globally, reinforcing your status as a reliable and well-managed business partner.

**Simplify Your Accounting:** A zero-rated supply under an LUT dramatically simplifies your bookkeeping. It removes the need for extra entries to track IGST payments, manage refund receivables, and reconcile the eventual return of funds. This leads to a cleaner, more accurate financial picture. The difference is stark:

rows: File a one-time annual LUT (Form GST RFD-11)
Track refund status with tax authorities
Working capital is locked until refund is received
Working capital remains in your account

body.content: Ultimately, leveraging the LUT transforms a recurring compliance task into a strategic advantage. It puts you in firm control of your cash, protects your valuable time, and enhances your professional standing.
Are You Eligible? A Simple Checklist

This strategic advantage is predicated on meeting a few straightforward eligibility requirements. The government designed the LUT facility to empower credible, compliant exporters. For the vast majority of professional service providers, these criteria are simple formalities. Think of it less as a hurdle and more as a confirmation that your business is structured correctly for global trade.

items: **You are registered under GST:** This is the foundational requirement. Your Goods and Services Tax Identification Number (GSTIN) is the key that unlocks the GST portal. If you provide services to clients outside India, you are required to have a GST registration, even if your turnover is below the standard threshold limits.

**You intend to export services without paying IGST:** The very purpose of the LUT is to facilitate "zero-rated supplies." If your business model involves invoicing international clients for consulting, software development, design, or other professional services, you directly qualify. The LUT is your formal declaration to the tax authorities of this intention.

**You have a clean compliance record:** This point often causes a flicker of anxiety, but the threshold for ineligibility is exceptionally high. To be ineligible, a person must have been *prosecuted* for tax evasion of an amount exceeding ₹2.5 crore (25 million rupees). This does not refer to a late filing or a minor clerical error, but to a formal, legal prosecution for significant tax fraud. For the typical independent consultant, this is a reassuringly high bar.

body.content: Your Pre-Flight Checklist: Gather These for a Flawless Filing

Once you've confirmed your eligibility, the next step is to prepare for a smooth filing. Think of this as organizing your flight plan before takeoff. Gathering a few key pieces of information beforehand transforms the process from a frantic search into a calm, five-minute task.
Here is what you'll need at your fingertips:

items: **Login Credentials for the GST Portal:** Have your username and password ready to access the government's official portal.

**Digital Signature Certificate (DSC) or Electronic Verification Code (EVC):** This is how you will formally sign your application. While a DSC (a physical token) is an option, the far more straightforward method for most professionals is the EVC. This option sends a One-Time Password (OTP) to your registered mobile number and email, allowing you to authenticate your identity instantly without special hardware.

**Details of Two Independent Witnesses:** You simply need the full name, occupation, and address of two individuals. They do not need to be notaries or government officials; they can be colleagues, clients, or other professional contacts. Having this information ready saves you the hassle of tracking it down mid-filing.

**A Self-Declaration Commitment:** You do not need to prepare a separate document. The online form will present you with three checkboxes. By ticking these, you formally declare that you will comply with all GST laws and complete your export within the prescribed time frames.

body.content: The Step-by-Step SOP for Filing Your LUT (Form GST RFD-11)

With your details assembled, the actual filing of Form GST RFD-11 is a refreshingly straightforward process. Think of the following as a simple Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) you can execute in minutes to secure your financial workflow for the entire year.

items:
**Step 1: Navigate to the Service**
    Log in to the official GST Portal. Go to **Services > User Services > Furnish Letter of Undertaking (LUT)**. This direct path takes you exactly where you need to be.

**Step 2: Select the Financial Year**
    The system will display Form GST RFD-11. Your first input is to select the correct financial year from the drop-down list (e.g., '2025-26' for the period from April 2025 to March 2026). An LUT is valid for one financial year.

**Step 3: Complete the Self-Declaration and Witness Details**
    This is where your preparation pays off. Tick the three checkboxes for the self-declaration, affirming your commitment to comply with GST regulations. Next, enter the Name, Occupation, and Address for the two independent witnesses whose details you gathered earlier.

**Step 4: Sign and File the Application**
    The final step is authorization. Select the EVC option, which will trigger an OTP sent to your registered mobile number and email address. Entering this OTP authenticates your identity and officially completes your filing.

**Step 5: Download Your Acknowledgment**
    The moment you file, the portal generates an Application Reference Number (ARN) and displays a confirmation message. Your final, critical action is to click the **DOWNLOAD** button to save the acknowledgment PDF. This document is your official proof of compliance. Save it immediately in a dedicated folder for your tax records.

body.content: Pitfall-Proofing Your LUT: Common Mistakes to Avoid

Executing the steps correctly is crucial, but true mastery lies in avoiding the subtle missteps that can undermine your efforts. Think of this as the final layer of your financial armor—the details that ensure your zero-rated status remains unbreachable.

items:
**Mistake 1: Filing for the Wrong Financial Year**
    The LUT is valid only for a single financial year (April 1st to March 31st). A frequent oversight is forgetting to file a new LUT on or after April 1st, creating a serious compliance gap. Any invoices raised in this gap period are not covered, meaning you would be legally required to pay IGST and then undertake the burdensome refund process.
    
    **The Pro-Tip Solution:** Systematize your filing. Make it a non-negotiable task in the first week of April every year. By making this a priority Q1 task, you ensure seamless business continuity.

**Mistake 2: Incorrect Witness Information**
    The portal does not verify witness information in real-time, which can create a false sense of security. However, inaccurate details can become a significant problem during a departmental audit. An officer could question the validity of your declaration if the witnesses are untraceable. This is not just about filling fields; it's about providing credible testimony.

**Mistake 3: Forgetting Post-Filing Obligations**
    Filing the LUT is the beginning of the compliance journey, not the end. The undertaking you sign is a promise that you will receive payment for your services in convertible foreign exchange within one year from the date of your export invoice. Failure to meet this condition retroactively invalidates the LUT's benefit for that specific transaction, making you liable for the 18% IGST plus interest.

body.content: As Chartered Accountant Varun Gupta of VARUN AMITA GUPTA & CO. puts it, "The compliance that truly validates an 'export of services' claim happens offline. The most critical, and often overlooked, step is meticulously securing the Foreign Inward Remittance Certificate (FIRC) or Bank Realisation Certificate (BRC) for every single overseas transaction. Without this documentary proof, in the eyes of the GST authorities, there is no export."
From Task to System: Automating Your Annual LUT Compliance

That discipline of connecting your on-portal promise with your off-portal proof is where a robust system becomes your greatest asset. The goal is to eliminate human error by building a framework that makes timely compliance an automatic, low-effort outcome.

items: **Set a Recurring Calendar Reminder:** Create an annual, recurring event in your digital calendar for April 1st titled "File Annual GST Letter of Undertaking (LUT)." Set two reminders: one for a week in advance and another for the day itself. The first nudge gives you time to confirm witness details; the second ensures the task is top-of-mind.

**Create a "Compliance SOP" Document:** As the CEO of your own enterprise, build systems for consistency. Create a simple Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) in your knowledge base (Notion, Google Docs, etc.). Title it "Annual LUT Filing Process" and include a direct link to the GST Portal, the details of your preferred witnesses, and a note to download the ARN acknowledgment. This turns a vague task into a repeatable process.

**Build it into Your Q1 Financial Checklist:** Every business-of-one should conduct a quarterly financial review. Make "Verify LUT Status for New Financial Year" a mandatory checklist item for your Q1 (April-June) review. This elevates the LUT from a standalone tax task to a core component of your financial health check-in, serving as a final backstop to safeguard your operations.

body.content: Frequently Asked Questions
Building a robust framework removes most friction from compliance, but astute questions will always surface. Here are the most common questions that arise around the Letter of Undertaking.

items:
**LUT vs. IGST Refund: Which is better for a service professional?**
    For a service professional whose lifeblood is cash flow, the **LUT is definitively the superior option**. The alternative—paying 18% IGST upfront and claiming a refund—locks up your working capital for weeks or months. The LUT allows you to export as a "zero-rated supply" from the outset, keeping your capital working for you.

rows: **None.** You do not pay IGST on your export invoice.
**Required.** You must pay the full IGST amount.
**Protected.** Your cash remains in your bank account.
**Blocked.** Your funds are tied up until the refund is processed.
**Minimal.** A single annual filing of Form GST RFD-11.
**High.** Requires filing refund claims for each transaction.

items:
**What is the time limit for export after filing an LUT?**
    For services, you must receive payment in convertible foreign exchange within **one year** from the date you issue the export invoice. Failing to meet this deadline for a specific transaction means you must pay the IGST amount plus interest.

**Can I file an LUT after the financial year begins?**
    Yes, you can file an LUT at any point during the financial year. However, it is only effective from the date of filing. Any export invoices issued *before* you file the LUT for that year will not be covered, requiring you to pay IGST and claim a refund for those transactions.

**Who can be a witness for a GST LUT?**
    You need two independent individuals. They simply need to provide their full name, occupation, and address. There is no requirement for them to be a notary or a Chartered Accountant. They can be colleagues, friends, or other professionals not directly associated with your business.

**What happens if I forget to renew my LUT?**
    If you forget to renew your LUT, any export invoices you issue will not qualify as zero-rated supplies. You will be legally obligated to charge and pay IGST on those services, forcing you into the refund process the LUT is designed to prevent.

**Is an LUT mandatory for exporting services?**
    No, it is not mandatory. The LUT is an optional facility to simplify compliance. The alternative is to pay the applicable IGST on your export invoice and subsequently file for a refund. The LUT is a strategic choice to improve your financial efficiency.

body.content: Conclusion: From Anxious Task to Empowered Operation

The strategic choice to use a Letter of Undertaking elevates your practice from a series of freelance gigs into a sophisticated, global operation. The process is not merely about paperwork; it's a powerful annual ritual that solidifies your control over your business's most critical resource: cash flow.

By treating this as a non-negotiable part of your financial operating system, you fundamentally change your relationship with compliance. It ceases to be a source of recurring anxiety and becomes a clear signal of your professionalism. You move from a position of uncertainty—wondering about refund timelines and blocked capital—to one of complete authority. You have a system, a checklist, and a calendar reminder.

This documented, repeatable process ensures your engagement in the export of services is seamless and financially efficient. It demonstrates a mastery over the tax framework that builds self-trust and reassures your international clients that they are dealing with a true professional. This is the essence of building a resilient business-of-one. You are no longer just reacting to the demands of clients or the complexities of government portals. Instead, you are proactively designing a business built for longevity and peace of mind. The Letter of Undertaking is far more than a form; it's a declaration of your intent, a testament to your operational maturity, and a vital tool that keeps your capital working for you.
